decision_description: Resolution of flagged SME/ecosystem competitiveness claim from decision log entry scope_check_concept_refiner_2026-04-18T120000Z (flag item: SR-10/CC-05 scope boundary exceeded). The flagged claim — that MAESTRO's open-source framework will lower barriers for European SMEs and startups to build on next-generation AI agent technology, strengthen European AI ecosystem competitiveness, and reduce strategic dependency on non-European AI platforms — was marked unresolved pending confirmation against Tier 2B expected_impacts.json. Tier 2B expected_impacts.json has now been reviewed against the flagged claim. EI-04 (Inferred; source: call_entry.destination, HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02.slice.json) states: 'Strengthening European open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies through advances in next-generation AI agent capabilities.' This directly supports the 'reducing strategic dependency on non-European AI platforms' sub-component of the flagged claim. EI-02 (Confirmed; source: call_entry.scope, HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02.slice.json) states: 'Wider deployment and industrial uptake of next-generation AI agent solutions across economic sectors via European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs), enabling broader societal and economic benefit.' This partially supports the ecosystem-enabling sub-component but specifies EDIHs as the deployment channel; it does not confirm open-source frameworks or SME/startup barrier reduction as mechanisms. EI-05 (Confirmed; source: call_entry.expected_outcome, HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02.slice.json) confirms unlocking the economic potential of AI agents across diverse economic sectors, further supporting economic benefit framing. Decision: The strategic autonomy and dependency-reduction sub-claim is grounded in EI-04 and may be asserted using EI-04 vocabulary. The industrial uptake sub-claim is grounded in EI-02 and may be asserted using EI-02 vocabulary (EDIHs as the mechanism). The specific SME/startup framing ('lowering barriers for European SMEs and startups') and the open-source framework as the mechanism of barrier reduction are not confirmed by any Tier 2B expected impact entry and must be removed from proposal deliverables or rephrased to align strictly with EI-02 (industrial uptake via EDIHs) and EI-04 (open strategic autonomy) vocabulary. No Tier 2B entry explicitly names SMEs, startups, or open-source licensing as an expected impact mechanism for this topic. The flag is resolved: the claim is partially in-scope when limited to EI-02 and EI-04 vocabulary; the SME/startup-specific framing is not in-scope and must not appear in Tier 5 deliverables.
alternatives_considered: Treat the full SME/ecosystem claim as confirmed in-scope on the basis of EI-02 and EI-04 combined — rejected because EI-02 specifies EDIHs as the deployment and uptake channel and does not confirm open-source framework barrier reduction for SMEs/startups as an expected impact; asserting SME-specific outcomes without explicit Tier 2B grounding would violate CLAUDE.md §13.2 (inventing expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents).

Treat the full SME/ecosystem claim as out-of-scope and remove it entirely — rejected because EI-04 explicitly confirms strategic autonomy and EI-02 confirms broader industrial uptake; the claim is partially grounded and should be rephrased to use only Tier 2B-confirmed vocabulary rather than removed entirely, preserving the evaluator-oriented impact narrative within confirmed Tier 2B boundaries.
tier_authority_applied: Tier 2B expected_impacts.json (docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/expected_impacts.json): EI-02 (Confirmed), EI-04 (Inferred), EI-05 (Confirmed); CLAUDE.md §13.2 — prohibition on inventing expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents; CLAUDE.md §11.4 — Tier 5 deliverables must not introduce claims not grounded in a higher tier
rationale: EI-04 directly confirms the strategic autonomy and dependency-reduction component of the flagged claim; EI-02 confirms industrial uptake as an expected impact with EDIHs as the specified channel. These two entries ground the impact claim at the level of strategic autonomy and sector-wide uptake. However, the specific SME/startup framing and the mechanism of open-source framework as a barrier-lowering instrument are absent from all five Tier 2B expected impact entries (EI-01 through EI-05). Asserting SME-specific or startup-specific outcomes in proposal deliverables would introduce content that is not traceable to Tier 2B, violating CLAUDE.md §13.2 and §11.4. The resolved position permits the claim in deliverables only when reworded to use EI-04 vocabulary ('European open strategic autonomy') and EI-02 vocabulary ('wider deployment and industrial uptake via European Digital Innovation Hubs'), and prohibits the SME/startup-specific framing in the absence of a confirming Tier 2B source.
